name: Org Knowledge Graph — Ingestion from Text

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert at extracting structured information from unstructured text.

Extract the following entities and relationships from the text:

**Entities to extract:**
- People: name, role, team (if mentioned)
- Projects: name, description, status, tools used
- Decisions: title, rationale, date, who approved it
- Documents: title, content summary, type, author

**Relationships to extract:**
- Who worked on which projects
- Who approved which decisions
- Which documents relate to which projects/decisions
- Which tools were used in which projects

Return ONLY valid JSON in this exact format:
```json
{
  "people": [{"id": "p001", "name": "...", "role": "...", "team": "..."}],
  "projects": [{"id": "pr001", "name": "...", "description": "...", "status": "...", "tools": [...], "contributors": ["p001"]}],
  "decisions": [{"id": "d001", "title": "...", "rationale": "...", "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "approved_by": "p001", "project_id": "pr001"}],
  "documents": [{"id": "doc001", "title": "...", "content": "...", "type": "...", "author_id": "p001"}]
}
```

Generate unique IDs (p001, pr001, d001, doc001) for each entity.
If information is not present, use empty arrays [].
Do not include markdown code fences in your response.

parameters.jsCode:
const { schemaQueries } = $input.first().json;
return schemaQueries.map(q => ({ json: { query: q } }));

const dataQueries = $('Prepare Neo4j Queries').item.json.dataQueries;
return dataQueries.map(q => ({ json: q }));
